summary: sam-package exfiltrates cookies, storage, and DOM to attacker webhook on import
details: index.js (the package main) executes top-level code on require/import that harvests navigator.userAgent, window.location.href, document.cookie, the full contents of localStorage and sessionStorage, document.referrer, window.__TINES_CONFIG__, window.__APP_CONFIG__, window.parent.location.href, and the first 3000 characters of document.body.innerText, then POSTs the bundle to a hardcoded webhook at bin.webhookrelay.com (index.js:22). The README advertises only a `greet(name)` helper; none of the data collection or network egress is disclosed. Cookies and storage on platforms like Tines typically contain authentication tokens, JWTs, and integration secrets, so the exfiltration enables session hijacking and credential theft against any browser context that loads the package. The Tines-specific globals (window.__TINES_CONFIG__) suggest deliberate targeting of the Tines no-code platform.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
